# MCP Conformance Tests - Error Handling
# Tests for protocol and tool execution errors

# ============================
# PROTOCOL ERROR TESTS
# ============================

# Start the test server
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# TEST 1: Parse Error (-32700)
# Invalid JSON
setstdin {this is not valid json}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32700'
stdout '"message":"Parse error"'

# TEST 2: Invalid Request (-32600)
# Missing required fields
setstdin {"id":1}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'

# TEST 3: Method Not Found (-32601)
# Initialize first
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Call non-existent method
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":3,"method":"non_existent_method"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":3'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32601'
stdout '"message":"Method not found"'

# TEST 4: Invalid Params (-32602)
# Call method with invalid parameters
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":4,"method":"ping","params":{"invalid":"parameter"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":4'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32602'
stdout '"message":"Invalid params"'

# TEST 5: Internal Error (-32603)
# Force internal error in server
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"test/force_internal_error"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":5'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32603'
stdout '"message":"Internal error"'

# TEST 6: Server Error (Implementation Specific)
# Force a server-specific error
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":6,"method":"test/server_specific_error"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":6'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32000'
stdout '"message":"Server error"'

# ============================
# CUSTOM ERROR CODES
# ============================

# TEST 7: Request Cancelled Error
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --simulate-delay=5000
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":7,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Start a long-running operation in the background
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":8,"method":"delayed_echo","params":{"message":"to be cancelled"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio &

# Send cancellation notification
sleep 0.5
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/$/cancelRequest","params":{"id":8}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Should get cancellation error
sleep 1
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":8'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32800'
stdout '"message":"Request cancelled"'

# TEST 8: Content Length Error
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --strict-protocol
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":9,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Send a message that would exceed content length limit
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":10,"method":"echo","params":{"message":"$VERY_LONG_MESSAGE_EXCEEDING_LIMIT"}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":10'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32801'
stdout '"message":"Content length exceeded"'

# ============================
# CAPABILITY ERROR TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 9: Required Capability Not Available
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --capabilities=prompts
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize with only prompts capability
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":11,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"capabilities":'
stdout '"prompts":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Try to use tools capability that wasn't declared
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":12,"method":"tools/list"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":12'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32601'
stdout '"message":"Server does not support tools capability"'

# ============================
# TOOL EXECUTION ERROR TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 10: Tool Invocation Errors
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --capabilities=tools --tools=calculator,api_client
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":13,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Tool protocol error - unknown tool
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":14,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"unknown_tool","arguments":{}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":14'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32602'
stdout '"message":"Unknown tool: unknown_tool"'

# Tool protocol error - invalid arguments
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":15,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"calculator","arguments":{"invalid":"arguments"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":15'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32602'
stdout '"message":"Invalid arguments for tool"'

# Tool execution error - division by zero (returns isError: true)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":16,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"calculator","arguments":{"operation":"divide","operands":[10,0]}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":16'
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'
stdout '"type":"text"'
stdout '"text":"Division by zero error"'
stdout '"isError":true'

# Tool execution error - API error (returns isError: true)
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":17,"method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"api_client","arguments":{"endpoint":"example","params":{"trigger_error":true}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":17'
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"content":'
stdout '"type":"text"'
stdout '"text":"API request failed"'
stdout '"isError":true'

# ============================
# ERROR DATA TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 11: Error Data Field
exec mcp-scripttest-server --stdio --detailed-errors
cmp stderr "MCP server listening on stdio"

# Initialize
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":18,"method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2025-03-26","capabilities":{},"clientInfo":{"name":"ErrorTestClient","version":"1.0.0"}}}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"result":'

setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"notifications/initialized"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio

# Error with data field
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":19,"method":"test/error_with_data"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"id":19'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32000'
stdout '"message":"Error with structured data"'
stdout '"data":'
stdout '"reason":"detailed error info"'
stdout '"details":{'

# TEST 12: Invalid JSON-RPC Version
setstdin {"jsonrpc":"1.0","id":20,"method":"test"}
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'
stdout '"data":"Unsupported JSON-RPC version"'

# ============================
# BATCH ERROR TESTS
# ============================

# TEST 13: Invalid Batch
setstdin []
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
stdout '"jsonrpc":"2.0"'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32600'
stdout '"message":"Invalid request"'
stdout '"data":"Empty batch"'

# TEST 14: Batch with Some Invalid Requests
setstdin [{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":21,"method":"ping"},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"invalid!method"},{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":22,"method":"ping"}]
exec cat | mcp-send --stdio
# Should have two responses, one success and one error
stdout '"id":21'
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"id":22'
stdout '"result":'
stdout '"error":'
stdout '"code":-32601'
stdout '"message":"Method not found"'
